title: Do you think Anno did drugs
display_title: Do you think Anno did drugs
wikitext:
[]: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Dec 15, 2015 10:34 pm Think about it, the idea of breaking down your AT field is a lot like LSD.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by TheAdmiral » Tue Dec 15, 2015 11:05 pm chaosakita wrote: Think about it, the idea of breaking down your AT field is a lot like LSD.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 16, 2015 12:45 am He dropped LSD, but he never inhaled. Wait...

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Dec 16, 2015 1:00 am Based on the impression of him and the society he lives in, I'd say almost certainly not. You don't need drugs to experience ego death anyway.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 16, 2015 1:05 am ^ Maybe Anno is the drug. Sorry, I don't know how seriously this is supposed to be taken. I personally don't think he did drugs for basically the same reasons Chuckman specified. That, and if he did drugs we'd probably never hear from him again.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 16, 2015 1:06 am Antidepressants, maybe.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Dec 16, 2015 1:39 am Alcohol? Caffeine?

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Wed Dec 16, 2015 6:40 am Coffee does appear rather frequently in NGE

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Dec 16, 2015 7:31 am And beer, for that matter!

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by cyharding » Wed Dec 16, 2015 12:36 pm Moyoco did say in Insufficient Direction that he liked to drink lots of beer, which led in part to him being so heavy for a time.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Dec 16, 2015 4:02 pm Some of the most sober people have made some of the most surreal cracked out crap, and some of the most drugged out strung out people have made some of the most straight laced serious stuff you've ever seen. Hunter S Thompson, anyone?

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 16, 2015 4:12 pm For us sober people, art is how we try to experience illegal drugs. Why take LSD when I can make a trippy movie?

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Wed Dec 16, 2015 4:26 pm I'm so sick of this "if something is creative, it must have been influenced by drugs" thing. I've been seeing it over and over again for most of the time I've been alive.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 16, 2015 4:52 pm Tumbling Down : The meme seems to arise from a fundamental misunderstanding of how many creative minds work. I also find the nonspecificity of the word "drug" to produce a fair amount of irony. I mean, I assume hallucinogenics are what was meant, but it's hard for someone like me to hear "this show is so weird, the creator must've used drugs", since I've been put on drugs to make me more "normal". Far from assisting the creative juices, psychiatric drugs tend to stem them instead.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Wed Dec 16, 2015 4:54 pm Reichu wrote: Tumbling Down : The meme seems to arise from a fundamental misunderstanding of how many creative minds work. I also find the nonspecificity of the word "drug" to produce a fair amount of irony.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Moap » Wed Dec 16, 2015 10:56 pm A lot of the scenes in NGE almost directly tie in to common themes/hallucinations Ive heard others report and some that I've seen myself whilst under the influence of strong hallucinogens such as LSD and Mushrooms. However, these same hallucinations can be induced by being sleep deprived, starved, or in a survival situation; of course many of these are no doubt onset by the body's release of DMT, that's still a lot different than smoking it yourself. My point is that you can trip hard without ever touching drugs, a shaman I know claims he has trip feelings after intense yoga sessions, and tons of people have claimed to me they can get to an alternate state of mind through meditation. Also as far as I know Japan's society is much less accepting of drugs than the US, so I think it's extremely unlikely that Anno is or ever was a regular drug user. Did he try them and draw inspiration from it? Maybe, I've always liked to think the water droplet cuts are an LSD reference.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 16, 2015 11:04 pm Moap wrote: I've always liked to think the water droplet cuts are an LSD reference.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Moap » Wed Dec 16, 2015 11:24 pm Reichu wrote: I've heard that they're a reference to the Japanese creation myth, which seems a little more germane to NGE's themes than LSD.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 16, 2015 11:28 pm Anno may not have taken any drugs, but he damn near created one. A helplessly addicting dive into the psyche, full of Freudian symbols and rapidly flashing images that leave you in altered state of mind and questioning who you are by the end of it, complete with random flashbacks years after the fact? The only thing missing is a broken family, bankruptcy, and a lifetime of regret, but I still have years of merchandise collecting before I can completely rule that one out.

[]: Re: Do you think Anno did drugs?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 16, 2015 11:31 pm The answer to this question is hidden in a cave... A cave hidden deep somewhere in a virgin island discovered by three children set adrift after an intense battle at sea. Yes, I see the answer there, deep inside that hidden island cave, and it is mushrooms! Mushrooms eaten by Shinji Higuchi in the absence of Hideaki Anno.
